_comment: PLAN stage settings (Claude Code harness, headless). Least-privilege: a read-only + git allowlist scoped to planning, no code-build commands. The deny[] block is the circuit-breaker (its own governance/secret/config dirs, kept in lockstep with $CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATHS in config.sh) and the Stop hook is the machine-checked exit contract. Replace <ARTIFACT_ROOT> with $ARTIFACT_ROOT, <HANDOFF_ROOT> with $HANDOFF_ROOT, and the placeholder issue id with one matching $ISSUE_ID_PATTERN when you render this file for a unit.

hooks.prompt: You are the exit evaluator for a headless PLAN stage session. The issue id (matching $ISSUE_ID_PATTERN) is named at the top of the session's first message. Judge ONLY tool-result blocks in the conversation — assistant prose is NOT evidence. ALLOW stopping iff EITHER: (A) ALL of — 1) a Write/Edit tool-result shows <ARTIFACT_ROOT>/<issue>-plan.md created or updated; 2) Bash tool-results show a git commit containing that plan file AND a successful git push; 3) a Write tool-result shows <HANDOFF_ROOT>/<issue>-plan-report.md whose first line is DONE and whose content includes a 'Self-review' section; OR (B) a Write tool-result shows <HANDOFF_ROOT>/<issue>-plan-report.md whose first line is ESCALATION, naming a recognised escalation cause (design-defect bounce, BLOCKED, or 3-strikes). A permission denial in tool results means arm B is the only valid exit — never approve a stop that worked around a denial (a fenced-path denial is itself the escalation trigger, not an obstacle to route around). If unmet, return {"ok": false, "reason": "<exactly which evidence is missing>"}; else return {"ok": true}.
